subject: Chemistry
solution:
In the given reaction, only the carbonyl group has been reduced while the carbon- carbon double bonds are unaltered.

This is possible with the help of Wolf Kishner Reduction whose reagent set is given in Opiton (C).

To reduce the carbonyl groups into alkane wolf - kischner reduction is used, without affecting the double bond.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The colour obtained is the colour of the compound formed in the reaction of aniline with NaNO2​/HCl at 0oC followed by coupling with β-naphthol.

Aniline on reaction with NaNO2/HCl forms a diazonium salt. This diazonium salt is then coupled with β-naphthol.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Lattice energy is defined as the amount of energy required to separate one mole of solid ionic compound into its gaseous ions. Evidently greater the lattice energy, higher is the melting point of the alkali metals halide and lower is its solubility in water.

L.E.∝M.P.

L.E. : LiF>LiCl, MgO>NaCl

Thus, I3- has trigonal bipyramidal geometry and linear shape .

Shape : Linear, I-I-I Bond angle ⇒180°

solution:
Clean water would have BOD value of less than 5 ppm whereas highly polluted water could have a BOD value of 17 ppm or more.

For survival of aquatic life dissolved oxygen is responsible its optimum limit 6.5 ppm and optimum limit of BOD ranges from 10-20 ppm & BOD stands for biochemical oxygen demand.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
For the following ion/compounds

The main difference between aromatic antiaromatic and nonaromatic is that aromatic means having a delocalized pi electron system with (4n +2) electrons and antiaromatic means having a delocalized pi electron system with 4 electrons whereas nonaromatic means there is no delocalized electron system in that molecule.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The process of conversion of primary aromatic amines into its diazonium salt is called diazotization. 

Diazonium salts are important synthetic intermediates that can undergo coupling reactions to form azo dyes and electrophilic substitution reactions to introduce functional groups.

The correct sequence of reagents used for converting nitrobenzene into m- dibromobenzene is found in option (b).

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Buna--S, 'S' stand for styrene.

Buna – S is a more commonly used name for a polymer named styrene butadiene rubber. The structure of Buna – S is given by:

Buna – S is formed using the monomers butadiene and styrene.

Thus in Buna – S, the Bu stands for Butadiene, since butadiene is one of the constituent monomers of the given polymer. Also, Na stands for sodium and S represents the monomer Styrene used for the synthesis of the given polymer.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
According to the Bohr's atomic theory, the kinetic energy of an electron is directly proportional to the square of the atomic number and inversely proportional to the square of the principal quantum number. 

(A) KE=13.6z2n2eVatom⇒KEαz2n2

(B) speed of e-αzn

∴  v×nαz

(C) Frequency of revolution of e-=v2πr

∴ Frequency αz2n3

(D) Coulombic force of attraction on the electron is directly proportional to the square of the atomic number and inversely proportional to the fourth power of the principal quantum number. 

 F=kq1q2r2=kze2r2  rαn2z

⇒ Fαzn2z2

⇒  Fαz3n4

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Blood is a negatively charged sol.

Blood being a colloidal solution, its coagulation by Hardy-Shuze's law states " Higher the charge on cation higher will be its efficiency to coagulate the colloidal solution" 

According to Hardy-Schulz's rule, the cation with high charge has high coagulation power.

FeCl3 has Fe+3 ions.
